title: Build and Execute REST Requests Knowledge Check

questions.question: Why are nonce tokens (CSRF tokens) required for POST, PATCH, and DELETE requests in WRS?
options: To encrypt the data being transmitted between client and server
To prevent replay attacks by ensuring requests cannot be resubmitted
To compress large JSON payloads for faster transmission
To authenticate the domain configuration on the Windchill server
questions.rationale: Nonce tokens prevent replay attacks where a malicious client stores signed-in requests and resubmits them later. The randomly generated strings expire after 24 hours. Nonces do not encrypt data, compress payloads, or authenticate domain configurations.

questions.question: What is the difference between an Object Reference (OR) and a Version Reference (VR) in Windchill?
options: OR identifies a specific object while VR always points to the latest iteration
OR is used for documents while VR is used for parts
OR includes version history while VR does not
OR is used in URL construction while VR is used only in response bodies
questions.rationale: An Object Reference (OR) identifies a specific object instance, while a Version Reference (VR) always leads to the latest iteration in a branch for iterated objects. Both can be used for parts and documents, both appear in URLs and responses, and neither stores version history by itself.

questions.question: What happens when a WRS request returns a 403 Forbidden status code?
options: The URL syntax is incorrect and needs to be reformatted
The nonce token has expired and must be refreshed
The user does not have the necessary access permissions for the operation
The domain version is incompatible with the entity set
questions.rationale: A 403 Forbidden error means the user lacks the necessary authorization for the operation, just as in the Windchill user interface. Incorrect URL syntax returns a 400, an expired nonce may return 401, and domain version issues are separate from access control.

questions.question: What format must the body of a SetState request use to change a life cycle state?
options: A plain text string with the state name
An XML element with state attributes
A JSON object with Display and Value keys inside a State property
A URL-encoded query string with the target state
questions.rationale: The SetState action requires a JSON body with the State property containing both Display and Value keys, such as {"State": {"Display": "Released", "Value": "RELEASED"}}. Plain text, XML, and URL-encoded formats are not accepted by WRS.

questions.question: What does the Postman Visualizer use to render response data as HTML?
options: SQL query templates
Handlebars templates with {{property}} syntax
CSS stylesheets applied to raw JSON
Built-in Postman chart components
questions.rationale: The Postman Visualizer uses Handlebars templates to filter and render JSON/XML responses into human-readable HTML formats. The template uses {{property}} syntax to extract values. It does not use SQL, CSS styling of raw JSON, or built-in chart components for custom visualizations.
